title: All Against All to All Help All

origin_context.observed_behavior: public hoarding of food and essential goods
origin_context.core_observation: In a crisis, fear-driven self-protection can rapidly produce scarcity, distrust, and escalating social fragmentation.

core_thesis.statement: When crisis escalates, society moves toward one of two behavioral attractors: all against all or all help all. The decisive variable is not material scarcity alone, but the collective shift in thought and behavior.

problem_definition.crisis_trigger: external shock producing fear, uncertainty, and perceived scarcity
problem_definition.systemic_risk: self-protective behavior amplifies scarcity and accelerates distrust
problem_definition.terminal_risk: if unchecked, competitive survival behavior can degrade into all against all

behavioral_sequence_negative.name: fear_activation
behavioral_sequence_negative.description: Individuals experience uncertainty and begin to prioritize private survival.

behavioral_sequence_negative.name: hoarding_behavior
behavioral_sequence_negative.description: Individuals purchase or secure more than they immediately need.

behavioral_sequence_negative.name: artificial_scarcity
behavioral_sequence_negative.description: Goods become unavailable not only because of supply limits, but because of defensive accumulation.

behavioral_sequence_negative.name: social_distrust
behavioral_sequence_negative.description: People begin to assume that others will act selfishly, and therefore imitate the behavior.

behavioral_sequence_negative.name: escalation_to_all_against_all
behavioral_sequence_negative.description: The social field shifts from coordination to competition, and crisis becomes amplified by human behavior.

behavioral_sequence_positive.name: fear_recognition
behavioral_sequence_positive.description: Individuals recognize fear without letting it govern behavior.

behavioral_sequence_positive.name: shared_responsibility_activation
behavioral_sequence_positive.description: People understand that their own security depends on the stability of others.

behavioral_sequence_positive.name: need_based_distribution
behavioral_sequence_positive.description: Resources are handled according to actual need rather than defensive accumulation.

behavioral_sequence_positive.name: trust_reinforcement
behavioral_sequence_positive.description: Visible acts of restraint and help reduce panic and stabilize group behavior.

behavioral_sequence_positive.name: transition_to_all_help_all
behavioral_sequence_positive.description: The social field shifts from competitive survival to cooperative survival.

three_insights.application: No person has a morally superior claim to hoard more than needed at the expense of others.
Crisis behavior must be governed by a deeper recognition of shared human dependence.

three_insights.axiom: We have a shared responsibility for each other.
three_insights.application: The practical alternative to all against all is all help all.

EX-TI.application: Even under pressure, survival behavior must not collapse into abandonment of the other.

constraints.rule: Do not treat excessive accumulation as true security when it creates insecurity for others.
constraints.rationale: Private overprotection can destabilize the collective environment on which private survival depends.

constraints.rule: In crisis, survival must be modeled as a shared system rather than isolated individual competition.
constraints.rationale: A society cannot remain stable if each person acts as though all others are threats.

constraints.rule: A crisis requires not only external measures but a transformation in thought and behavior.
constraints.rationale: Without internal behavioral change, external systems become overloaded by fear-driven action.

signals.description: Sudden mass purchasing of essential goods beyond normal need.
Scarcity grows because of human behavior rather than supply limits alone.
People assume that others will act selfishly and therefore imitate selfish behavior.
Individuals voluntarily limit consumption or accumulation to preserve access for others.
People actively support others in practical ways during crisis conditions.

decision_logic.primary_intervention: activate shared responsibility before scarcity becomes identity-based conflict

ai_role.function: Preserve and recall the behavioral distinction between all against all and all help all during crisis interpretation.

canonical_statement: The decisive moral question in crisis is whether fear turns people against each other or responsibility turns them toward each other.
